| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109404491/550990802/-889231773?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_ToughMap_250611_T1_1_a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYmNmNmU4NC1mOTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NwWTV7ZxfCuq05fWszJlDGB7zuKCHXDJEOKOGor6V0w=) Derek, this is a long email about the state of the Senate map – and why your support for Mark Warner’s reelection campaign has never been more important.

If you’re short on time, and already convinced that we need to protect Mark’s seat in the Senate, you can make a donation right [_**here.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109404493/550990805/-889231773?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_ToughMap_250611_T1_1_a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYmNmNmU4NC1mOTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NwWTV7ZxfCuq05fWszJlDGB7zuKCHXDJEOKOGor6V0w=) But if you have the time, we hope you’ll give this a read. _____________ There’s no sugarcoating it. Democrats face a tough map to take back the Senate next year: **Roll Call: “Senate Democrats need to break some streaks to win the majority”

NBC News: “Democrats face an uphill climb to take control of the chamber in the 2026 elections”

Saboto’s Crystal Ball: “Our Initial Senate Ratings: Republicans Start as Strong Favorites to Hold Majority”** With retirements in key battleground states – New Hampshire, Michigan, and Minnesota – and Republican incumbents defending only two battleground states, Democrats will need to hold every one of our current seats and flip traditional Republican strongholds to win the seats needed to take back the majority. _____________ Not to mention, Republicans are not just preparing to hold their majority, they think they can expand it.

Tim Scott, chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee, namechecked Virginia as an opportunity to net an additional Senate seat.

They are looking at the same data we’re looking at: Virginia shifted right in 2024, and with the right opponent, we could be a top battleground state again. _____________ If we don’t show early, strong support for Mark Warner, Republicans could take an even closer look at Virginia.

That’s why our June fundraising numbers are so important. At the end of the month, we’ll file a public report with the FEC. Any signs of weakness, and we could see a Republican billionaire sweep in with a massive super PAC against us.

We did the math, and we need an average of 345 donations every single day to stay on track with our fundraising goals. Unfortunately, today has been a bit slower than we expected.

[_**That’s why your donation today – not tomorrow, not next week, not next year – really does make all the difference. We need to show Republicans how strong our campaign is before this month’s end-of-quarter deadline. Will you be one of the 345 donors we need before midnight to help us do it?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109404495/550990808/-889231773?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_ToughMap_250611_T1_1_a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYmNmNmU4NC1mOTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NwWTV7ZxfCuq05fWszJlDGB7zuKCHXDJEOKOGor6V0w=) Hey Peter, Now that the weather's getting warmer in Vermont, it’s finally starting to feel like summer. But our work in the House isn't slowing down any time soon. I've heard from Vermonters across the state who are concerned about Trump's dictator-like crackdowns, disastrous billionaire-first budget, and dangerous disregard for due process. I want to hear from you, too. I wish we could talk face-to-face, Peter, but this survey is the best way for us to check in.** [Can you take a minute or two to share what’s on your mind?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109404997/550992378/-570838756?contactdata=Za4z2VwAwk0836xAjWZN+t1hV/YBrCnBR17RSKZQtSn+UKrE5cu7atF6NsmtIchh8NkSTw8Bf2C1xtuUPU9rPHN/T/RiziFWxJ6zvYTHKexuPJHXUgqB/wQRtXVWv4nAe6LQWDn06Jyt9CKbQy02d4EMZvm8QPkKUUyaUQ5KswU=&refcode=AU_RAB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NDk0YzhhMS1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Wd3WPSxP--OaEisJaobJyD82sXl2NhfXYz_rMAfPE7Y=&emci=3dfb7ced-2946-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=5494c8a1-fa46-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=43402272)** | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109404998/550992379/-570838756?contactdata=Za4z2VwAwk0836xAjWZN+t1hV/YBrCnBR17RSKZQtSn+UKrE5cu7atF6NsmtIchh8NkSTw8Bf2C1xtuUPU9rPHN/T/RiziFWxJ6zvYTHKexuPJHXUgqB/wQRtXVWv4nAe6LQWDn06Jyt9CKbQy02d4EMZvm8QPkKUUyaUQ5KswU=&refcode=AU_RAB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NDk0YzhhMS1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Wd3WPSxP--OaEisJaobJyD82sXl2NhfXYz_rMAfPE7Y=&emci=3dfb7ced-2946-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=5494c8a1-fa46-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=43402272)

In Congress, I'm doing everything I can to stand up to the Trump administration and their Republican enablers. But I also want to empower folks like you to use your voices and demand better.
By **[sharing your thoughts today](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109404999/550992380/-570838756?contactdata=Za4z2VwAwk0836xAjWZN+t1hV/YBrCnBR17RSKZQtSn+UKrE5cu7atF6NsmtIchh8NkSTw8Bf2C1xtuUPU9rPHN/T/RiziFWxJ6zvYTHKexuPJHXUgqB/wQRtXVWv4nAe6LQWDn06Jyt9CKbQy02d4EMZvm8QPkKUUyaUQ5KswU=&refcode=AU_RAB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NDk0YzhhMS1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Wd3WPSxP--OaEisJaobJyD82sXl2NhfXYz_rMAfPE7Y=&emci=3dfb7ced-2946-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=5494c8a1-fa46-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=43402272)** , you’re helping me better address the impacts of this regressive and lawless administration.
Thanks for making your voice heard.

I want to hear from you, too. I wish we could talk face-to-face, Peter, but this survey is the best way for us to check in. | In Congress, I'm doing everything I can to stand up to the Trump administration and their Republican enablers. But I also want to empower folks like you to use your voices and demand better. By , you’re helping me better address the impacts of this regressive and lawless administration. body:
I must say - this one stings, Fellow Patriot...
I woke up to the unfortunate news that we came up short of our May end-of-month goal. We were on pace until the very last 48-72 hours, then fundraising dropped off a cliff in the final sprint.
I'm disappointed. Not in you (never in you) but in myself.
Whether I didn't explain the urgency well enough or my emails were missed, regardless, we failed. And it hurts.
Why? Because the Democrats are already circling my race like sharks.
\- They named me as their ONLY House Republican target in all of Texas \- TX-15 is one of just THREE seats they need to take back the House
\- If they win here, they'll impeach Trump and block his entire agenda
We CANNOT let this shortfall become a trend.
[That's why I'm coming to you, with my hat in hand, to ask for a small contribution to get us back on track. name: Ryan Crosswell
subject: Donald Trump asked me to ignore the law. I said no.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio House Democratic Campaign Committee, 545 E Town St
body:
My name is Ryan Crosswell, and today, I’m officially launching my campaign to represent Pennsylvania’s 7th District in Congress.

|
---
Donald Trump asked me to ignore the law. I said no. Every citizen who cares about American democracy must have a line in the sand that cannot be crossed. My name is Ryan Crosswell, and today, I’m officially launching my campaign to represent Pennsylvania’s 7th District in Congress — because Donald Trump crossed my line. [**If Donald Trump has crossed your line, please consider splitting a donation between Ohio House Dems and my campaign to help me reach my Launch Day Digital Goal. Every dollar we raise sends a message to Donald Trump that this is our country, and we’re going to fight for it.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405153/550992770/-576296379?refcode=250611_Crosswell_Honor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWJhZWVlNC1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pAvZZmfHar4pkbAUD7k1nGcUfW3EQnXpMVJghNXBclU=) | **[DONATE NOW>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405154/550992771/1810887588?refcode=250611_Crosswell_Honor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWJhZWVlNC1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pAvZZmfHar4pkbAUD7k1nGcUfW3EQnXpMVJghNXBclU= "https://secure.actblue.com/")**
---
I grew up in Pottsville, Pennsylvania. Motivated to serve because of 9/11, I joined the Marine Corps, where I learned the code of Honor, Courage, and Commitment.
I kept that code during my time as a federal prosecutor under Presidents Obama, Biden, and Trump.
I locked up child predators, took on drug traffickers, and took down corrupt politicians — until the day Donald Trump decided to weaponize the Department of Justice to go after his political opponents and let his friends off the hook.
He wanted the prosecutors in my section to drop a case against his political ally. He wanted me to break the code and ignore the law, and that’s something I would never do.
**So I said no and resigned from the Department of Justice — then, I fought back.**
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405155/550992772/-576296379?refcode=250611_Crosswell_Honor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWJhZWVlNC1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pAvZZmfHar4pkbAUD7k1nGcUfW3EQnXpMVJghNXBclU=)**
I testified publicly in a congressional hearing about the threat Donald Trump’s agenda poses to our justice system, the integrity of the federal government, and our democracy itself.
I don’t have the deep pockets of the big corporations that are ripping us off while hardworking Pennsylvanians fight like hell just to get by, or the influence of the corrupt politicians who keep the game rigged against everyday Americans, or the power Trump has forcibly seized for himself.
But that won’t stop me from fighting back against him again — with Honor, Courage, and a Commitment to the real people and families of Pennsylvania’s 7th.
[**I’d be honored to have you by my side in this fight for the long haul. If you’re all in to flip the House blue and put a real check on Donald Trump’s power in 2026, I’m personally asking you to split a donation between Ohio House Dems and my campaign today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405156/550992773/-576296379?refcode=250611_Crosswell_Honor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWJhZWVlNC1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pAvZZmfHar4pkbAUD7k1nGcUfW3EQnXpMVJghNXBclU=)
| [**$15 »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405157/550992774/1810887588?amount=15&refcode=250611_Crosswell_Honor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWJhZWVlNC1mYTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pAvZZmfHar4pkbAUD7k1nGcUfW3EQnXpMVJghNXBclU= "https://secure.actblue.com/")

clean_body: My name is Ryan Crosswell, and today, I’m officially launching my campaign to represent Pennsylvania’s 7th District in Congress. | Donald Trump asked me to ignore the law. I said no. Every citizen who cares about American democracy must have a line in the sand that cannot be crossed. My name is Ryan Crosswell, and today, I’m officially launching my campaign to represent Pennsylvania’s 7th District in Congress — because Donald Trump crossed my line. = | = "" I grew up in Pottsville, Pennsylvania. Motivated to serve because of 9/11, I joined the Marine Corps, where I learned the code of Honor, Courage, and Commitment. I kept that code during my time as a federal prosecutor under Presidents Obama, Biden, and Trump. I locked up child predators, took on drug traffickers, and took down corrupt politicians — until the day Donald Trump decided to weaponize the Department of Justice to go after his political opponents and let his friends off the hook. He wanted the prosecutors in my section to drop a case against his political ally. He wanted me to break the code and ignore the law, and that’s something I would never do. So I said no and resigned from the Department of Justice — then, I fought back. = I testified publicly in a congressional hearing about the threat Donald Trump’s agenda poses to our justice system, the integrity of the federal government, and our democracy itself. I don’t have the deep pockets of the big corporations that are ripping us off while hardworking Pennsylvanians fight like hell just to get by, or the influence of the corrupt politicians who keep the game rigged against everyday Americans, or the power Trump has forcibly seized for himself. But that won’t stop me from fighting back against him again — with Honor, Courage, and a Commitment to the real people and families of Pennsylvania’s 7th. = | = "" | = "" | = "" | = "" Thank you. Ryan Crosswell = = OHDC 545 E. Town St. Columbus, OH 43215 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . name: Angie Craig
subject: This is a longer email, but I hope you’ll read it until the end.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Growing up, there weren’t a lot of folks like me in public office. Peter, this is a longer email, but it’s an important one, too — so I hope you’ll take a few minutes to read until the end.

Growing up, there weren’t a lot of folks like me in public office. I grew up in a small rural town and didn't come out to my family until college. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405343/550993335/713703037?refcode=em25_250611_aganod_fndd_gq_act___AC_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250611_aganod_fndd_gq_act___AC_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=1e324273-fb46-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZTMyNDI3My1mYjQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9tbSUoBchFmoKzSraYLA8e9pKvYtj-HEQb53c694ClM=) Back then, I could hardly imagine that someone like me could run for office — not to mention flip a Republican-held congressional district.

It can be scary to take a swing and run for office, especially in a world where some people don’t support families or marriages like mine and my wife, Cheryl’s.

Against the odds — with _lots_ of help from committed and open-minded Minnesotans — I became the first lesbian mom to serve in Congress in 2018. I’m the first lesbian Mimi (grandmother) to serve in Congress, too!

**Now, I’m running for U.S. Senate to continue serving Minnesotans.**

I’m not running for office just to make history. I’m running so I can help Minnesota families — the ones who look like mine and Cheryl’s, and those who don’t.

Donald Trump and his allies in the Senate want to divide us, but at the end of the day, most working families across America have more in common than not. Most folks just want a good-paying job, a safe, welcoming community and affordable everyday costs.

[**I’m ready to fight to make that a reality for every American, but first, I need your help to win this toss-up race. Can you split $10, or any amount, between Kim's and my campaign for U.S. Senate to start this race clean_body: Growing up, there weren’t a lot of folks like me in public office. Peter, this is a longer email, but it’s an important one, too — so I hope you’ll take a few minutes to read until the end. Growing up, there weren’t a lot of folks like me in public office. I grew up in a small rural town and didn't come out to my family until college. = Back then, I could hardly imagine that someone like me could run for office — not to mention flip a Republican-held congressional district. It can be scary to take a swing and run for office, especially in a world where some people don’t support families or marriages like mine and my wife, Cheryl’s. Against the odds — with lots of help from committed and open-minded Minnesotans — I became the first lesbian mom to serve in Congress in 2018. I’m the first lesbian Mimi (grandmother) to serve in Congress, too! Now, I’m running for U.S. Senate to continue serving Minnesotans. I’m not running for office just to make history. I’m running so I can help Minnesota families — the ones who look like mine and Cheryl’s, and those who don’t. Donald Trump and his allies in the Senate want to divide us, but at the end of the day, most working families across America have more in common than not. Most folks just want a good-paying job, a safe, welcoming community and affordable everyday costs. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- I’m honored to have folks like you on my team to help me win this next fight. Thank you, Angie = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress PO Box 2728 Issaquah, WA 98027 United States

name: Sarah Kline
subject: Peter, it’s go time for our transportation package!
body:
https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2?am=20&contactdata=8drS1twgDESST6YOtWd7w5qBvui0w67Pbi+WcTzNS/kXIhIclAHV38ONUKamqHZdSNs+CbPKpTRp2qvQHVjknZ7HIeIwtZnDIWIKfmfxOv+z3+dsSIgdrDPmp7Vq7pVE [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2?am=20&contactdata=8drS1twgDESST6YOtWd7w5qBvui0w67Pbi+WcTzNS/kXIhIclAHV38ONUKamqHZdSNs+CbPKpTRp2qvQHVjknZ7HIeIwtZnDIWIKfmfxOv+z3+dsSIgdrDPmp7Vq7pVE]
Hi Peter,
Big news: the Legislature has finally unveiled the long-awaited transportation package [https://www.oregonlegislature.gov/wagner/Documents/Oregon%20TRIP%20to%20Invest%20in%20Roads,%20Bridges,%20Transit%20and%20Provide%20Oversight%20of%20ODOT.pdf] ( HB 2025 [https://olis.oregonlegislature.gov/liz/2025R1/Measures/Overview/HB2025] )! While this package includes several things we advocated for – like Safe Routes to School and wildlife crossings – some important items are missing, including dedicated funding for transportation electrification. Without climate investments, this package will take us backwards on climate. This is a critical moment; we’re all hands on deck turning out volunteers to testify at hearings this week and we need your help. Will you make an urgent donation to OLCV today to help? [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2]
Donate now [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2?am=20&contactdata=8drS1twgDESST6YOtWd7w5qBvui0w67Pbi+WcTzNS/kXIhIclAHV38ONUKamqHZdSNs+CbPKpTRp2qvQHVjknZ7HIeIwtZnDIWIKfmfxOv+z3+dsSIgdrDPmp7Vq7pVE]
This session you’ve shown up tirelessly in support of a transportation package that works for ALL Oregonians. With the help of grassroots supporters like you, the package includes important programs like Great Streets and Safe Routes to School, and funding for wildlife crossings and transit. One thing that is not included, however, is investments in electrification, which is essential for us to reduce toxic air pollution and meet our climate goals ( read more here [https://www.moveoregonforward.org/news/for-immediate-release-oregon-transportation-bill-wins-on-safety-falls-short-on-vehicle-electrification-and-off-street-trails] ). https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2?am=20&contactdata=8drS1twgDESST6YOtWd7w5qBvui0w67Pbi+WcTzNS/kXIhIclAHV38ONUKamqHZdSNs+CbPKpTRp2qvQHVjknZ7HIeIwtZnDIWIKfmfxOv+z3+dsSIgdrDPmp7Vq7pVE [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2?am=20&contactdata=8drS1twgDESST6YOtWd7w5qBvui0w67Pbi+WcTzNS/kXIhIclAHV38ONUKamqHZdSNs+CbPKpTRp2qvQHVjknZ7HIeIwtZnDIWIKfmfxOv+z3+dsSIgdrDPmp7Vq7pVE]
There’s still time left to make sure that the transportation package includes climate investments in electrification like incentives for electric trucks or vehicles and charging infrastructure. We’re recruiting folks to testify at hearings this week and we need your help now.
Will you help by donating now? Your support ensures that we have the resources we need to mobilize OLCV members to testify in support of a climate friendly transportation package. [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2]
Renew your membership [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2?am=20&contactdata=8drS1twgDESST6YOtWd7w5qBvui0w67Pbi+WcTzNS/kXIhIclAHV38ONUKamqHZdSNs+CbPKpTRp2qvQHVjknZ7HIeIwtZnDIWIKfmfxOv+z3+dsSIgdrDPmp7Vq7pVE]
Transportation not only connects us, but is also a lifeline for many people. We deserve a transportation system that works for all of us, and reflects our state’s values and commitments to our environment, climate, and communities. But the clock is ticking on our ability to get this done.
OLCV, our members, and our partners, have been working together for over a year to ensure the future of transportation reflects our values. The work isn’t done, but if all of us contribute however we’re able to now, I believe we can achieve a transportation package that reflects all of our values. [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2]
Renew your membership [https://secure.everyaction.com/TDc4a2r_QEugjeZtF01ipw2?am=20&contactdata=8drS1twgDESST6YOtWd7w5qBvui0w67Pbi+WcTzNS/kXIhIclAHV38ONUKamqHZdSNs+CbPKpTRp2qvQHVjknZ7HIeIwtZnDIWIKfmfxOv+z3+dsSIgdrDPmp7Vq7pVE]
Thank you for your support,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Sarah%20Signature.png]
Sarah Kline (she/they)
OLCV Communications & Membership Coordinator
P.S.: If you’d no longer like to receive fundraising emails, please use this link here [https://secure.everyaction.com/p/24dczWT--Uqtcu_3MxYYog2] to log-in and update your email preferences.
https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv [https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv] https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org [https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org] https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F [https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F] https://x.com/OLCV [https://x.com/OLCV]

Oregon League of Conservation Voters
1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/ReVeUSnqh0mAa9rjhFdl7A2?unsubscribedata=nDC0shuXS4WWTefvldmZsAxGdhfKaXjdG27b63m%2Bl4iNPGHUB%2B9L2Exh5xAHSBlo5KECnourOS36QLsHvWgqd8pyuQHAHf3%2F72zW5ZDBxYSvz9GRqqM2Z4Kd4TOonPozuSulWEoSM511d3OnrmmhvE%2FOiw3%2F2FBvvuqZDuAzTWDcs2hib2tL3dXNLdSp7oP1tUzbJOVfiQrAIMEAc0YzihNT0eV7aB%2BNfsvuYUVGA%2FbsKSjzlm7g7JXtFeArBx25qXNX9E1CmYzznjcaGgO6pKxm6T1GZ017mP%2F9YN7bgljBxmMP9MbrA1jxEp%2FRx%2Fs7y5PYU1PS3UQ3LTR5lwa234jnRf4ANtTu8Gn9eTcu1WX1Wlct7gtuMD%2FbqbK3oCb6TyLgR7ged827yk7kTobf4FUQrPN9x6QvvlMwE%2BpCvbM%3D .

name: Robert Garcia
subject: Truth, Justice, and the American Way
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Robert Garcia for Congress
body:
With your help, I’ll be Trump’s kryptonite
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405430/550993598/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMDQyYy1mYzQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PWNEKrAKJobmbqSSH2CF-OKza6k6qSpKmFgGmpn4inA=)
Hi there, it’s Robert Garcia — the Democrat representing California’s 42nd District in Congress.
My favorite superhero, Superman, has a slogan: **"Truth, Justice, and the American Way."**
It’s a phrase that represents the core values he embodies: honesty, fairness, and freedom — and it’s a phrase I try to live my life by.
**It’s that ethos that drove me to run for Congress. And it’s that same ethos that drives me every day in my efforts to hold the Trump Administration accountable.**
While Donald Trump and his lawless administration trample on our Constitution and the rule of law, it’s never been more critical to have strong Democratic leadership that’s not afraid to stand up for Truth, Justice, and the American way.
That’s the kind of leader I’ve always been — and I’ve vowed to be Donald Trump’s kryptonite so long as I hold my position.
[**But I need you with me. So today I’m asking you to pitch in $20 or more as a sign of support for my efforts to be Donald Trump’s kryptonite.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405431/550993599/-309737922?refcode=250611_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMDQyYy1mYzQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PWNEKrAKJobmbqSSH2CF-OKza6k6qSpKmFgGmpn4inA=)
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405432/550993600/-309737922?refcode=250611_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMDQyYy1mYzQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PWNEKrAKJobmbqSSH2CF-OKza6k6qSpKmFgGmpn4inA=)
Republicans in Congress refuse to do their job. **Instead of honoring their oaths of office and performing the kind of oversight needed, they’re turning a blind eye as Donald Trump leads us into a constitutional crisis.**
Enough is enough. We need to fight back like the future of our democracy depends on it — because it does.
[**So, please, if you’re ready to join me in this fight, donate now and let’s work together to fight for the future.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405433/550993601/-309737922?refcode=250611_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMDQyYy1mYzQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PWNEKrAKJobmbqSSH2CF-OKza6k6qSpKmFgGmpn4inA=)
Thank you,
Robert
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405434/550993602/-309737922?refcode=250611_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMDQyYy1mYzQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PWNEKrAKJobmbqSSH2CF-OKza6k6qSpKmFgGmpn4inA=)

name: Yassamin Ansari
subject: 15 million Americans could lose healthcare coverage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress
body:
|
---
Peter, Yassamin here. House Republicans have already passed Trump’s “One Big Beautiful Bill” — you know, the one that will rip healthcare away from millions of Americans just to bankroll billionaire tax cuts — now, it’s up to the Senate to push the bill into law. Here’s what’s on the table: \- Nearly 15 million Americans could lose health insurance
\- $3.75 trillion in tax cuts for the wealthy
\- A $2.4 trillion explosion to the federal deficit This bill is as cruel as it is corrupt. And yet Republicans are rushing to get it to Trump’s desk by July 4th, because what says “freedom” like ripping away healthcare while handing billionaires another payday? I’m **_not_** in Congress to rubber-stamp Trump’s attacks on working people. I’m here to fight for affordable healthcare, real economic security, and a future where everyone can thrive (not just the 1%). I’m in Congress to fight for YOU, Peter. ****But to keep fighting, I need you with me. This campaign doesn’t take a dime of corporate PAC money. We rely on people like you to pitch in where they can. If you’re able, please chip in today: _**[secure.actblue.com/donate/ya-em-jun25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405463/550993747/-2009428703?amount=&refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNmM0NTc1Yy1mYzQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8ztpRt5oI1r7NryEYD-dV9O_4sxEDg5XmPgMKOA9buQ=)**_**** We can stop this if we work together. — Yassamin

* * *
Yassamin Ansari for Congress
PO Box 13524
Phoenix, AZ 85002
United States ****[yassaminforcongress.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109405464/550993748/1625509068?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNmM0NTc1Yy1mYzQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8ztpRt5oI1r7NryEYD-dV9O_4sxEDg5XmPgMKOA9buQ=&emci=db3a7855-fc46-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=f6c4575c-fc46-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=45157234) ** | [info@yassaminforcongress.com](mailto:info@yassaminforcongress.com)** | **Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress.**

clean_body: | Peter, Yassamin here. House Republicans have already passed Trump’s “One Big Beautiful Bill” — you know, the one that will rip healthcare away from millions of Americans just to bankroll billionaire tax cuts — now, it’s up to the Senate to push the bill into law. Here’s what’s on the table: \- Nearly 15 million Americans could lose health insurance \- $3.75 trillion in tax cuts for the wealthy \- A $2.4 trillion explosion to the federal deficit This bill is as cruel as it is corrupt. And yet Republicans are rushing to get it to Trump’s desk by July 4th, because what says “freedom” like ripping away healthcare while handing billionaires another payday? I’m not in Congress to rubber-stamp Trump’s attacks on working people. I’m here to fight for affordable healthcare, real economic security, and a future where everyone can thrive (not just the 1%). I’m in Congress to fight for YOU, Peter. **But to keep fighting, I need you with me. This campaign doesn’t take a dime of corporate PAC money. We rely on people like you to pitch in where they can. If you’re able, please chip in today: = We can stop this if we work together. — Yassamin Yassamin Ansari for Congress PO Box 13524 Phoenix, AZ 85002 United States ** | mailto:info@yassaminforcongress.com | Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress.

Friend, we already know that our race will be one of the most important congressional races in the country next year. National Republicans know Marcy has our back - and that’s why they are laser-focused on this race.

Dark money groups are already running attack ads against Marcy and lying about her record, and one potential Republican challenger has already announced their candidacy.

There is even talk of a rematch against our 2024 opponent, who was hand-picked by national Republicans and came within a fraction of a percentage point of winning this seat.

We beat them last year, friend, and we can beat them again — but we can't do it without your support.

* * *
# [WARNING: Enforced Disappearances Are Increasing](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165728752&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1NzI4NzUyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDk2NzE1OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1MjI2MzU5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BnwlHRL8y7WRHd2bLYJMj2Gbp4J8_v9pb1AaM8hX5Mw)
### An interview with scholar Dr. Jessica Mecellem on this expanding autocratic action--and how we as ordinary citizens can fight back to protect our civil rights
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jun 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165728752&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1NzI4NzUyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0OTY3MTU5OCwiZXhwIjoxNzUyMjYzNTk4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RsjnAiTks8MlUrb3Zb3T1pIBHD4S6slCgq4sBV-WDm8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165728752&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1NzI4NzUyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDk2NzE1OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1MjI2MzU5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BnwlHRL8y7WRHd2bLYJMj2Gbp4J8_v9pb1AaM8hX5Mw&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165728752&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1NzI4NzUyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDk2NzE1OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1MjI2MzU5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BnwlHRL8y7WRHd2bLYJMj2Gbp4J8_v9pb1AaM8hX5Mw)
---
| | [](https://substack..fdsiHqMcYxuybxctBXM_HxGraRj_gElKHDji5O_DiLU?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/warning-enforced-disappearances-are?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

The enforced disappearances of Mahmoud Khalil, Kilmar Abrego Garcia, Rumeysa Ozturk, and Andry Jose Hernandez Romero are but a snapshot of the hundreds of known cases of enforced disappearances committed by this Trump regime. Such actions resemble an autocracy quickly spiring into fascism, rather than a Democratic Republic that protects due process of all for all people in this country--as the 5th and 14th Amendments mandate. How do we respond to this growing threat?
Today I am pleased to share with you an interview with Dr. Jessica Mecellem, PhD, an independent scholar who studies enforced disappearance as a state practice. This is an important conversation for my readers because by better understanding how enforced disappearances are a hallmark of authoritarian regimes, we can better understand how to identify these injustices in real time, and work to stop and prevent them from happening in the future. Education is our weapon against injustice, and I'm grateful to Dr. Mecellem for sharing her wisdom with us. **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..6ZZVwdxuuwbuYadw8S1qllC1LZ5g5OSqZ41SpS9Bozw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=165728752)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9a140eae-17e7-4409-b6fa-a28b13e2e664?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Credit: United Nations
* * *
**Qasim: Before we dive in, please share with us a little about yourself and your research on enforced disappearances?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** As a political scientist, I have researched how societies deal with violent and unjust pasts. Specifically, my research has focused on instances in which governments use enforced disappearance as a coercive practice to stifle dissent and create obedience. Scholars and journalists have [documented](https://substack.com/redirect/5771aad7-7433-4bbb-9ba2-fa04a4f9458a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) how this coercive practice is part of a set of techniques that has been taught by militaries to other militaries and police around the world.
I no longer work in academia, but I continue to work to increase awareness about this important topic. The strategy behind enforced disappearances is to convince the wider population that what they witness, or hear about others witnessing, is not actually happening. Therefore, increased public awareness and sharing of information about the step-by-step practice of enforced disappearance is one of our society's best lines of defense in this moment. What I share here comes from analysis of enforced disappearances in a range of countries over almost a century, including Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, the former Yugoslavia, Turkey, Algeria, Lebanon, as well as some earlier cases in the 20th century such as Nazi Germany's Night and Fog decree, and also in the context of Franco's Spain.
* * *
**Qasim: So let 's dive into the main question--what exactly is an enforced disappearance?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** According to the [Rome Statute](https://substack.com/redirect/835696fa-65a6-4266-b068-b6563fa84426?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which established the foundations of the International Criminal Court in 1998, enforced disappearance is a crime against humanity. Many human rights [organizations provide ample information](https://substack.com/redirect/38e3a62e-7fe0-4024-995d-06de4c33f4a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about it, but in the most simple terms, it occurs when three factors are in play:
1. a person is deprived of their liberty by agents of the State or by persons or groups of persons acting with the authorization, support or acquiescence of the State, followed by
2. a refusal to acknowledge the deprivation of liberty or by concealment of the fate or whereabouts of the disappeared person, which
3. place such a person outside the protection of the law.

The last part--placing them outside of the protection of the law--is key in understanding how this human rights abuse functions. When someone is forcibly disappeared, their loved ones and legal representation do not know where they are or cannot reach them to provide legal protection. According to this definition, as of today, [thousands of people have been forcibly disappeared in the United States](https://substack.com/redirect/cddaf77e-6f39-4ebe-8c39-8cc25a659b46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
**Qasim: What hallmarks of the practice are we seeing in the US today?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** There are a couple things to consider. Let me break them down.
Narratives: Public narratives promoted by people in power that out groups--in this case immigrants--are "dangerous", "criminal", "undeserving of constitutional rights" (that are legally theirs, simply for living on US soil, whether or not they are citizens). Those dehumanizing narratives seek to justify in the eyes of the wider population draconian measures that criminalize and brutalize entire communities.
States of exception: When the government leader claims that the circumstances are so exceptional that normal procedures that we would have to follow to uphold the rule of law, can be ignored, for public safety. But the key thing here is to observe - there is no evidence of an immigrant invasion being used to justify a state of exception.
Tactical Elements: The use of detention facilities that minimize legal access of detainees, the use of unmarked vehicles and or plain-clothed officers who are unwilling to identify themselves, even tacit encouragement or approval of vigilante violence or the participation of private security companies or militias in law enforcement activity, a refusal to tell families or lawyers where the person is being taken, under what authority, how to contact them. Makeshift and moving detention centers, and people being tracked in the detention system and then simply disappearing from that system, no longer being tracked. Quotas among law enforcement which incentivise arrests and reduce the incentives for officers to comply with the rule of law.
All of these things are hallmarks of enforced disappearances and are currently being documented in the US.
* * *
**Qasim: Why do state actors (or those working with their authorization or acquiescence) forcibly disappear people?**
At their core, enforced disappearances are an outcome of the deterioration of and disregard for the rule of law. And there's two main things to remember here:
* It is a[ tactic of terror](https://substack.com/redirect/5a991f34-38e0-4069-993e-7fd7949d7ef6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that impacts not only the person abducted, but the remaining community--those left behind. Because of the refusal to provide concrete information about who took the person, where they have been taken, or where they are being held, and because legal protection is rendered impossible in this situation, communities and families left behind are filled with fear. This form of [ambiguous loss](https://substack.com/redirect/65ef253d-57e8-4ed0-889e-c4bd774d6bbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) created by enforced disappearances has been recognized by international legal courts as [a form of torture](https://substack.com/redirect/2b7fac72-4c70-45fd-a753-94396ab36775?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This is important because the US has signed and ratified the [Convention against Torture and Other Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment](https://substack.com/redirect/fdca90bf-0ac5-44b2-9b86-7262fc1643d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Enforced disappearances are a silencing technique, to scare others into backing down in their criticism of those in power, as well as to paint those left behind in communities as social pariahs who cannot be trusted or who are not equally human and deserving of their constitutional rights.

**If you 're finding value in this insight and scholarship, join Let's Address This as a free or paid subscriber.**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..6ZZVwdxuuwbuYadw8S1qllC1LZ5g5OSqZ41SpS9Bozw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=165728752)
* * *
**Qasim: Has this happened in the US before? If so, how does what we are seeing now compare to that?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** In short, yes. Enforced disappearances have occurred in a variety of contexts in the United States in the past. For example, a non-exhaustive list includes:
* In [Chicago's Homan Square](https://substack.com/redirect/c69aaefb-a046-4f92-9dab-a1187d24249c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) which has been documented by the Guardian to be a location of enforced disappearances by the police department between at least 2012-2015 in which up to 7,000 individuals were disappeared.
* [Portland, Oregon in 2020](https://substack.com/redirect/1d1c7bc2-b995-4e02-bcc5-055686013750?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Federal and local law enforcement forcibly took protestors in unmarked vans.

Although these were time-bound instances in which individuals were released within hours or days, they are still considered enforced disappearance in international law. But there's more examples I can share:
* In 2017 a prison system was set up in Louisiana to house immigrants for deportation, which the[ ACLU has described in an in-depth report published in 2024](https://substack.com/redirect/20440ed0-3b52-469b-ba31-8c33ab83b0f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as mimicking enforced disappearance because of the ways that victims are prevented from the protection of the law. The organization[ Freedom for Immigrants demonstrated in their 2021 report](https://substack.com/redirect/eacae64e-e79e-4c21-bf63-6c65af980fc0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "enforced disappearances are a feature--not a bug--of the immigration detention system, which routinely and systematically disappears those in its custody."
* The decades long crisis of[ Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women](https://substack.com/redirect/b74b4c74-44a6-4cdb-a517-5d2c7db377bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [people across the United States](https://substack.com/redirect/cf3ac6e2-efa4-431b-866c-b2937018267c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is in many ways similar to and overlapping with the dynamics of enforced disappearance. Although there are particular historical and contemporary dynamics surrounding cases of MMIW&P which shouldn't be ignored, we should not overlook the similarities to enforced disappearance.
* The US government engaged in what is recognized as enforced disappearances during the War on Terror, through its practice of [extraordinary rendition](https://substack.com/redirect/8051f0af-8b49-4e24-bd0b-558d82afee67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Renditions have been adopted again by the current administration as a tactic used to remove immigrants to third party countries such as El Salvador, Costa Rica and Djibouti.

* * *
**Qasim: Are there any things or actions that protect against the use of enforced disappearances?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** Certainly, we should look to build up several aspects of our society that will better protect us from enforced disappearances. This includes, for example:
* Strong rule of law, including, crucially, a dependable and timely judicial response to instances of enforced disappearance.
* Civil society with an established system for "Rapid Action Alerts" to draw attention quickly to cases, spread the word and make it publicly unacceptable for perpetrators to continue.
* The professionalization of private security companies; reduction of overlapping jurisdiction of different agencies; strong ethical commitment to civil rights and liberties for all; whistleblower protections, etc.
* Strong positive histories of relations between law enforcement and civil society in a given geographic location which can facilitate trust between these groups, documentation of activity associated with enforced disappearances, and sanctioning of perpetrators.

* * *
**Qasim: When we talk about these cases of enforced disappearances, how many cases are we talking about? Are these exceptional circumstances that can be understood as being carried out by "bad apples"?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** Not exactly. It's more widespread than most people even realize, though fortunately more people are becoming aware of it today. One data scientist has put together[ this tracker](https://substack.com/redirect/071aa154-fbd8-4b27-ad15-26b71214b1af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), based on media reports. Although it's not perfect (it includes some cases that do not fall into the definition of enforced disappearance), it is gathering cases documented in the media and, according to those working on the tracker, it is an undercount of current cases. It should be noted that, those case documented on this tracker do not include the hundreds mentioned in the[ 2024 ACLU report](https://substack.com/redirect/20440ed0-3b52-469b-ba31-8c33ab83b0f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), nor the almost 1000 cases of individuals mentioned in the[ 2021 FIF report](https://substack.com/redirect/eacae64e-e79e-4c21-bf63-6c65af980fc0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which determined that "these enforced disappearances are a feature--not a bug--of the immigration detention system."
Remember: The main goal of enforced disappearance is to create a chilling effect on the community and families of those left behind--to scare them and confuse them into silence and retreat from criticism of the government, or participation in cultural or political activity that the government has deemed threatening. But we know this is a tactic - one that has been taught by militaries to other militaries and police and intelligence actors around the globe.
* * *
**Qasim: This seems daunting and is incredibly frightening. But I refuse to believe that we are helpless. What can we do to fight back in a meaningful way?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** You are right that while it is frightening, we are not helpless. Based on what organizations around the world of families of the disappeared have done in situations like these and significantly worse, here are some steps I encourage everyone to understand and be ready to take:
* Document what you're seeing even if it feels like there is no recourse right now
* Connect with local community organizations about how you can help spread the word
* Speak vehemently against what has already happened to spread awareness among the general population of the practice of enforced disappearance and what to do if it is witnessed.
* Refuse the minimizing discourse being advanced to justify this activity (dehumanizing, criminalizing, claiming state of emergency, etc.)
* Assess your understanding of where your city, town, or state falls in relation to key dynamics with police, the judiciary, etc.,
* Support local organizations representing targeted groups that have systems--[such as Freedom for Immigrants](https://substack.com/redirect/cbf26dcf-e062-460a-b014-6c5bcee6197b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--or that are setting up systems to document, investigate and rapidly publicize occurrences in the form of urgent action alerts, as well as toolkits [for families of those disappeared](https://substack.com/redirect/212b1e25-efa5-403c-a2e8-2af32045d19a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Support the funding of legal defense funds for cases going before the courts.

* * *
**Qasim: These are excellent ideas and at least for me, helps combat the feeling of helplessness or hopelessness. What do you want people to remember as they share this information with those around them?**
**Dr. Mecellem:** [Families around the world](https://substack.com/redirect/2c7a48cb-26c2-4be3-b1e3-977a5fac701f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) have been and continue to fight for truth, justice and repair in the face of enforced disappearances. It is because of the tireless work they have done that today international law captures this crime - recognizes it as an illegal human rights abuse, and a crime against humanity. Their work--documenting and increasing awareness about enforced disappearances, and researching to lead to repair--has won court cases in the European Court of Human Rights, the Latin American Court of Human Rights, and in front of judges of the International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia, to name only a few.
**Qasim: Thank you so much for your time today. We hope to have you back soon to continue to share your insights.**
**Dr. Mecellem:** It's my pleasure, and yes I would love to continue to collaborate on this incredibly important issue.
* * *
### Further information about Jessica Mecellem
Dr. Jessica Mecellem has published about Enforced Disappearances in the _[International Journal of Transnational Justice](https://substack.com/redirect/643d8b6b-97b7-4af3-9c1d-78bb06cf95da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ and _[Law& Social Inquiry](https://substack.com/redirect/44895fa8-704c-47fe-93a9-3cb0a5e4ebdb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. Her work has also been cited by the [International Committee of the Red Cross](https://substack.com/redirect/3cbf7e38-0691-4104-8ca6-6906eba2484b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
She holds a PhD in Political Science from Loyola University and has a master's degree from the Aix-Marseille University in Middle Eastern Studies. She currently works in the private sector and as an independent scholar. Readers can contact Dr. Mecellem [here](https://substack.com/redirect/9e8ab6de-7c66-45d1-a1b1-5ed9a8c9fc35?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

An interview with scholar Dr. Jessica Mecellem on this expanding autocratic action--and how we as ordinary citizens can fight back to protect our civil rights | | | Jun 11 | | | | | | | | | | | | The enforced disappearances of Mahmoud Khalil, Kilmar Abrego Garcia, Rumeysa Ozturk, and Andry Jose Hernandez Romero are but a snapshot of the hundreds of known cases of enforced disappearances committed by this Trump regime. Such actions resemble an autocracy quickly spiring into fascism, rather than a Democratic Republic that protects due process of all for all people in this country--as the 5th and 14th Amendments mandate. How do we respond to this growing threat? Today I am pleased to share with you an interview with Dr. Jessica Mecellem, PhD, an independent scholar who studies enforced disappearance as a state practice. This is an important conversation for my readers because by better understanding how enforced disappearances are a hallmark of authoritarian regimes, we can better understand how to identify these injustices in real time, and work to stop and prevent them from happening in the future. Education is our weapon against injustice, and I'm grateful to Dr. Mecellem for sharing her wisdom with us. Let 's Address This. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- Credit: United Nations Qasim: Before we dive in, please share with us a little about yourself and your research on enforced disappearances? Dr. Mecellem: As a political scientist, I have researched how societies deal with violent and unjust pasts. Specifically, my research has focused on instances in which governments use enforced disappearance as a coercive practice to stifle dissent and create obedience. Scholars and journalists have how this coercive practice is part of a set of techniques that has been taught by militaries to other militaries and police around the world. I no longer work in academia, but I continue to work to increase awareness about this important topic. The strategy behind enforced disappearances is to convince the wider population that what they witness, or hear about others witnessing, is not actually happening. Therefore, increased public awareness and sharing of information about the step-by-step practice of enforced disappearance is one of our society's best lines of defense in this moment. What I share here comes from analysis of enforced disappearances in a range of countries over almost a century, including Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, the former Yugoslavia, Turkey, Algeria, Lebanon, as well as some earlier cases in the 20th century such as Nazi Germany's Night and Fog decree, and also in the context of Franco's Spain. Qasim: So let 's dive into the main question--what exactly is an enforced disappearance? Dr. Mecellem: According to the , which established the foundations of the International Criminal Court in 1998, enforced disappearance is a crime against humanity. Many human rights about it, but in the most simple terms, it occurs when three factors are in play: 1. a person is deprived of their liberty by agents of the State or by persons or groups of persons acting with the authorization, support or acquiescence of the State, followed by 2. a refusal to acknowledge the deprivation of liberty or by concealment of the fate or whereabouts of the disappeared person, which 3. place such a person outside the protection of the law. The last part--placing them outside of the protection of the law--is key in understanding how this human rights abuse functions. When someone is forcibly disappeared, their loved ones and legal representation do not know where they are or cannot reach them to provide legal protection. According to this definition, as of today, . Qasim: What hallmarks of the practice are we seeing in the US today? Dr. Mecellem: There are a couple things to consider. Let me break them down. Narratives: Public narratives promoted by people in power that out groups--in this case immigrants--are "dangerous", "criminal", "undeserving of constitutional rights" (that are legally theirs, simply for living on US soil, whether or not they are citizens). Those dehumanizing narratives seek to justify in the eyes of the wider population draconian measures that criminalize and brutalize entire communities. States of exception: When the government leader claims that the circumstances are so exceptional that normal procedures that we would have to follow to uphold the rule of law, can be ignored, for public safety. But the key thing here is to observe - there is no evidence of an immigrant invasion being used to justify a state of exception. Tactical Elements: The use of detention facilities that minimize legal access of detainees, the use of unmarked vehicles and or plain-clothed officers who are unwilling to identify themselves, even tacit encouragement or approval of vigilante violence or the participation of private security companies or militias in law enforcement activity, a refusal to tell families or lawyers where the person is being taken, under what authority, how to contact them. Makeshift and moving detention centers, and people being tracked in the detention system and then simply disappearing from that system, no longer being tracked. Quotas among law enforcement which incentivise arrests and reduce the incentives for officers to comply with the rule of law. All of these things are hallmarks of enforced disappearances and are currently being documented in the US. Qasim: Why do state actors (or those working with their authorization or acquiescence) forcibly disappear people? At their core, enforced disappearances are an outcome of the deterioration of and disregard for the rule of law. And there's two main things to remember here: It is a that impacts not only the person abducted, but the remaining community--those left behind. Because of the refusal to provide concrete information about who took the person, where they have been taken, or where they are being held, and because legal protection is rendered impossible in this situation, communities and families left behind are filled with fear. This form of created by enforced disappearances has been recognized by international legal courts as . This is important because the US has signed and ratified the . Enforced disappearances are a silencing technique, to scare others into backing down in their criticism of those in power, as well as to paint those left behind in communities as social pariahs who cannot be trusted or who are not equally human and deserving of their constitutional rights. If you 're finding value in this insight and scholarship, join Let's Address This as a free or paid subscriber. Qasim: Has this happened in the US before? If so, how does what we are seeing now compare to that? Dr. Mecellem: In short, yes. Enforced disappearances have occurred in a variety of contexts in the United States in the past. For example, a non-exhaustive list includes: In which has been documented by the Guardian to be a location of enforced disappearances by the police department between at least 2012-2015 in which up to 7,000 individuals were disappeared. , Federal and local law enforcement forcibly took protestors in unmarked vans. Although these were time-bound instances in which individuals were released within hours or days, they are still considered enforced disappearance in international law. But there's more examples I can share: In 2017 a prison system was set up in Louisiana to house immigrants for deportation, which the as mimicking enforced disappearance because of the ways that victims are prevented from the protection of the law. The organization that "enforced disappearances are a feature--not a bug--of the immigration detention system, which routinely and systematically disappears those in its custody." The decades long crisis of and is in many ways similar to and overlapping with the dynamics of enforced disappearance. Although there are particular historical and contemporary dynamics surrounding cases of MMIW&P which shouldn't be ignored, we should not overlook the similarities to enforced disappearance. The US government engaged in what is recognized as enforced disappearances during the War on Terror, through its practice of . Renditions have been adopted again by the current administration as a tactic used to remove immigrants to third party countries such as El Salvador, Costa Rica and Djibouti. Qasim: Are there any things or actions that protect against the use of enforced disappearances? Dr. Mecellem: Certainly, we should look to build up several aspects of our society that will better protect us from enforced disappearances. This includes, for example: Strong rule of law, including, crucially, a dependable and timely judicial response to instances of enforced disappearance. Civil society with an established system for "Rapid Action Alerts" to draw attention quickly to cases, spread the word and make it publicly unacceptable for perpetrators to continue. The professionalization of private security companies; reduction of overlapping jurisdiction of different agencies; strong ethical commitment to civil rights and liberties for all; whistleblower protections, etc. Strong positive histories of relations between law enforcement and civil society in a given geographic location which can facilitate trust between these groups, documentation of activity associated with enforced disappearances, and sanctioning of perpetrators. Qasim: When we talk about these cases of enforced disappearances, how many cases are we talking about? Are these exceptional circumstances that can be understood as being carried out by "bad apples"? Dr. Mecellem: Not exactly. It's more widespread than most people even realize, though fortunately more people are becoming aware of it today. One data scientist has put together, based on media reports. Although it's not perfect (it includes some cases that do not fall into the definition of enforced disappearance), it is gathering cases documented in the media and, according to those working on the tracker, it is an undercount of current cases. It should be noted that, those case documented on this tracker do not include the hundreds mentioned in the, nor the almost 1000 cases of individuals mentioned in the, which determined that "these enforced disappearances are a feature--not a bug--of the immigration detention system." Remember: The main goal of enforced disappearance is to create a chilling effect on the community and families of those left behind--to scare them and confuse them into silence and retreat from criticism of the government, or participation in cultural or political activity that the government has deemed threatening. But we know this is a tactic - one that has been taught by militaries to other militaries and police and intelligence actors around the globe. Dr. Mecellem: You are right that while it is frightening, we are not helpless. body:
| | | Some folks write you emails pretending to be President Trump.

**I won 't do that to you. **

Some folks pretend donations will somehow fund DOGE.

**I prefer to be honest with you.**

This is Steve Scalise. As your House Majority Leader, I work hand and glove with President Trump, and I love what DOGE is doing for our country.

And when I ask you for support, I want to treat you with respect and be transparent about where your money is going.

You are not an ATM. You are a valued member of our MAGA movement. You deserve to be treated that way.

**_[So that's why I 'm dropping into your inbox right now:](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=cd990f024fc142d397867f37a07b2e2c&_e=3xbKpfOGzQxrW4k4Ckuu2elQmomNNfpDkNUqF_nmphIaSAuWDc-QmtZlbuR_etodZ5HxKhDywiJxvbOx01XK1E2o6sGZBzmeUcW4wELPxkDhCE76_DudTnEtlnqf3n6HZQ8NyS4IdxIUMbI_dCOoZpdWUQEvia_nJjlROFAy-XUHvuIDU7ZRi-SioKBQy0yCLCFrOncAT1LfojkM-M9beyQdr4xVtA8GvfkbzgmWxEjEi7zzGmdA1nvNhlmSpLXV52xEqbcSpCRCOz5bcM8yPJvz9rtMHLJfHz5IZlZgaXiUErniMi_97Oovp_AjTYPvCIblROu8q2FIb9NTkBDn2u57Zz891WwysSoUK1RMPo9gl72U1rKhayBpRjF9tfYFPsgBuPZBCUOVUjDRTIFC_A%3D%3D)_**

The Democrat Machine is firing on all cylinders to undermine, stonewall, and sabotage everything President Trump and DOGE are doing to make our government more efficient and cut waste.

Of course, the DC Swamp can't have that.

That's why they're mobilizing the courts, Deep State operatives, and Congressional Democrats to unleash a coordinated political hatchet job against the Trump Administration.

_Democrats are suing him. They 're pushing for partisan investigations. They're firing off hit piece after hit piece in the press. They're introducing legislation to block DOGE's access._

Their plan is simple:
**1.** Attack the Trump Administration
**2.** Drag them into court battles
**3.** Slow them down at every turn
**4.** Use the press to attack their work
**5.** Unload millions to win the Midterms
**6.** Block Trump's ENTIRE legislative agenda

clean_body: | | Peter—it’s LaMonica McIver. Last month, the Trump administration brought charges against me because I was doing my job as a member of Congress. Now, I’ve been officially indicted. Let me be clear: under the law, members of Congress are explicitly authorized to conduct oversight at ICE facilities. I was there to do my job and make sure that this administration isn’t abusing people’s rights. And I was charged and now indicted. The administration’s attacks are baseless and the facts are on my side, but that’s not stopping them from going after me.= These charges hold no factual merit. But there’s a reason they brought them: the Trump administration thinks they can intimidate me and scare me out of doing the job I was elected to do. They’re wrong. I need your help to send an unmistakable message: Americans won’t stand for this. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately and split between PA Dems and LaMonica McIver. | = | = | = | = | = | = We set a goal of 5,000 new grassroots donors to stand with us before our 11:59pm deadline TONIGHT. If we stand together we can amass the resources to mount a legal defense against this baseless indictment and show the Trump administration that the people who oppose his cruel agenda won’t be intimidated into silence. This is far bigger than one person. The rule of law is under attack, and what we do next matters. = We’re in this together, Rep. LaMonica McIver = | Follow us on , , and **! subject: Some encouraging news from the courts

body:
I'm personally counting on you to help keep up the momentum >>
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109406014/550996223/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MDUxOTBhZC1mZTQ2LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LwhVMICS8VXSk0Sh5t284twBdj9NsfcY6_PzKVzlDQ8=&emci=93bf49e9-d946-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=605190ad-fe46-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=21278485)
---
“House Democrats have filed 8 different briefs in court exposing both the illegality and the widespread harm of the Administration’s attacks on our people and our institutions. _Trump is losing in court every day and we are beating him._ Americans demoralized by the ceaseless authoritarianism of the Trump Administration need to know this.” – Congressman Jamie Raskin

I first met Rep. (and Constitutional scholar!) Raskin before I ran for office and I was just as impressed then as I am now. As we navigate this hard time when it sometimes feels like our system isn’t holding, I’m grateful for his leadership as the senior-most Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee.

Although Democrats are fighting back every way we know how, I want to highlight what’s happening in the courts, where many of the President’s actions are being stopped. As an engineer, I like data, so here are the facts:
| |
* Over 300 lawsuits have been filed challenging this Administration’s actions.

* Courts have issued over 170 orders blocking the Administration’s illegal and unconstitutional actions.

---
For example, in the last week alone, judges have:
| |
* Ordered the Administration to restore AmeriCorps and its funding.

* Blocked the resumption of deportations under the Alien Enemies Act.

* Ordered the Administration to reinstate fired employees at the Department of Education.

* Blocked the cancelation of student visas and blocked the President’s attempt to stop Harvard from enrolling students from other countries.

* Extended a freeze on the Administration’s mass firings at 22 agencies.

---
As the Administration continues to flood the airwaves with reckless and dangerous pronouncements and actions, it’s critical that we don’t lose track of the challenges they’re having enacting their agenda. All is NOT well and we’ll keep fighting with every tool in our toolkit. But as my friend, Jamie, said it’s important for Democrats to know that _“Trump is losing in the court every day and we are beating him.”_
There's work to be done,

Chrissy

---

P.O. Box 222
Devon, PA 19333

name: Tina for Oregon
subject: Such a disaster
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
The House recently passed Trump’s budget, Peter, and it’s a disaster for the American people. The GOP nicknamed it the “Big, Beautiful Bill” — which is funny, because it’s not beautiful. It’s just one big disaster. Here are just a few of the things the bill will do:
* **Impose Medicaid and SNAP cuts.** These cuts will force millions to lose their health insurance, and will rip food off the table for families who are struggling to get by.
* **Reduce clean energy tax credits.** This reduction could halt Oregon’s progress towards sustainable energy.
* **Increase the national debt.** According to _AP News_ , it will add $2.4 trillion to the national deficit.

**[Governor Kotek is committed to safeguarding the best interests of Oregonians, and that means calling on the Senate to vote NO on this bill. Add your name now to join her →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109406975/550999225/-1630788739?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODU5YzE2Yi0wMDQ3LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N5_z5FyMGmfA-2ecvA0TafrxAtUXVouG9g8Iu_QdO9Q=&emci=06b48865-0047-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=6859c16b-0047-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15353729)** | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109406976/550999226/-1630788739?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODU5YzE2Yi0wMDQ3LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N5_z5FyMGmfA-2ecvA0TafrxAtUXVouG9g8Iu_QdO9Q=&emci=06b48865-0047-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=6859c16b-0047-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15353729)

Tina made a promise to protect Oregon and our values from the chaos of the federal government.
But she needs as many grassroots supporters as possible standing with her, to add to the chorus of voices sending a message to the Senate.
**[Sign the petition now to stand with Tina against this budget →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109406977/550999227/-1630788739?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250611_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODU5YzE2Yi0wMDQ3LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N5_z5FyMGmfA-2ecvA0TafrxAtUXVouG9g8Iu_QdO9Q=&emci=06b48865-0047-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=6859c16b-0047-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15353729)**

name: Delia Ramirez
subject: It’s time for Congress to Block the Bombs.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

I just introduced H.R. 3565 — the Block the Bombs Act — a bold step to end
the U.S.’s complicity in the ongoing atrocities in Gaza.

This historic legislation would block Trump, and any future president,
from sending the most lethal offensive bombs and weaponry to the Israeli
government while it continues to violate international law and devastate
Palestinian communities.

Over 20 members of Congress are backing this bill. That’s a major shift —
and it’s happening because of the pressure our movement is building
together.

Since October 2023, the U.S. has sent $17.9 billion in military aid to
support Israel, including over 2,000 bombs — the same bombs used to level
entire neighborhoods and kill thousands of innocent civilians.

This moment demands courage. Congress has the power and responsibility to
act. I am ready to lead in this fight but I can’t do it alone.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250611-dr?refcode=em20250611-36751&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36751_1203867_J5jdh-&akid=36751%2E1203867%2EJ5jdh- ]This bill will make me a target of right wing groups like AIPAC. Will
you stand with me and show the strength of our movement by splitting a
donation between my campaign and Justice Democrats?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250611-dr?refcode=em20250611-36751&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36751_1203867_J5jdh-&akid=36751%2E1203867%2EJ5jdh- ]Contribute $5

The Block the Bombs Act is about more than just stopping American weapons
from being used in Gaza — it’s about our values. It’s about whether we
will continue funding human rights violations, or finally draw a line and
say: we refuse to be complicit in war crimes.

In solidarity,
Delia

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250611-36751-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36751_1203867_J5jdh-&akid=36751%2E1203867%2EJ5jdh-

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36751%2E1203867%2EJ5jdh-

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603

name: Brent Hennrich
subject: Breaking: Marie voted to glorify Trump’s deportation machine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
| | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMU_BeZcmRBr16dPjlrMKhhjiC51ataw3Peft1wIO87ITgHzbN-g_7vOEkTbFPjmJIKh3b2kqxHXJwog5pOzgpOQxG9qRw2J6lkm-9sVCLvAty14wEsUIGrHKGgfl0nxy17IK8BRk9IvqQECk9l-xU_HllQkTtLN5r2pZeKCB8XdSQ2T9F1mq2UBs84WUmSD4TN-9eJtVMi-FBTJBod6pjU_9iT1K5aQIiirtqaWMPsSTk6D_0DhdbOHCP0dodfV9PC0nljB_Ws-MVg848dk0hII/4h9/PmSPaGD7SX6xRlgact4FnA/h0/h001.ziDhh7FRpGZgxwo9dRSGo04YwwcrTy838mBpZp4A1lU)
---
Peter,
I can't believe I'm writing this but here we are:

**Marie Gluesenkamp Perez just supported House Resolution 488, a Republican-led measure glorifying Trump 's mass deportation regime and expanding ICE's unchecked power.

**Let me repeat that: She backed a resolution glorifying Trump's mass deportation regime, right as that same regime turns Marines and the National Guard against their own civilians.
If you think ICE has been targeting "criminals" in Los Angeles I've got a story for you.

ICE agents have raided Home Depots. They've raided the historic garment district in Los Angeles, following tax records to sewing shop workers who were literally on the job.

**You know, people paying taxes. Unlike the billionaire class Marie just cozied up to at her DC**** _WelcomeFest_****appearance.

** You can't claim to stand with working people while rubber-stamping the playbook of the same right-wing extremists trying to purge them from this country.

I believe in public safety. But we don't get that by letting ICE play judge, jury, and executioner. We need due process. We need leadership that doesn't support disappearing people for a headline.

Marie voted for the SAVE Act. She voted for the Laken Riley Act. And now she's thrown her weight behind House Resolution 488: three bills that attack immigrants, criminalize families, and spread the debunked lie that immigration is fueling the fentanyl crisis.

**Let 's call this out for what it is: These are not just bad votes, it's clear and indisputable evidence that Marie Gluesenkamp Perez is virulently anti-immigrant and completely in sync with Trump's racist immigration enforcement.

**I'm running because _this_ is the line in the sand.

**You 're either standing firm for the rule of law, due process for all, and fighting for the people - or you're siding with the jackboots.

**Marie made her choice. I've made mine.

Now, Democratic primary voters get to make theirs.

I won't go to DC to play footsie with fascists or make excuses for state-sponsored cruelty.

I'll go to fight for working people, the ones ICE is targeting while Marie keeps siding with the folks writing the orders.

[**If you 're ready for a Democrat who doesn't flinch when it counts, chip in now.

**](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUBVBi5luJvfPXRkl8M9UoQ7ZBbtwDc8anRVkYpKcmW_LoqSiRLg2kzT63KE86ybggfcMGLY360d3MNP-IfJald2EnX14FHKLVR2-B_s_vm5tCoELYSGHxd7CEA2QnS1ySeEG-CAGkWZI_nWJJo8YYNa3iuC_DRj0kVPf6nWOECLGpa_2dIojKrf65Vwrp4PbxIb0xIK7BPOZwWHEFuRXMlB7CEmCCojAn3iXGS1M_ibwf0CHER9dPtKnitq6_d8QiWA2pBJaFgpZb5Q0QzE-522ntKmsz-_PliI7i8eMo15PaPpetYIfEfSPvQKx40DMm4/4h9/PmSPaGD7SX6xRlgact4FnA/h1/h001.swa8HFzNIxWcLoKrWMqf7Nn-xNyg9vv8k3wNbEpYcn0)Let's Heal Washington!

Brent
